patches.find: Independent of the scientific critique above, this claim also fails our post-hoc test on structural grounds. The numerical value is textbook physics (Part 3.1b, "trivial-transcription test"): it appears in undergraduate references and widely indexed resources, so there is no meaningful sense in which the dome monitor script "predicted" it before reading the data. Under the dome author's own definition ("prediction was timestamped before confirming data was pulled"), the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable. No legitimate-novelty carve-out applies: the value is not obscure and the derivation (where one is offered at all) is algebraically equivalent to a standard-model derivation. See Part 3.1b.
patches.replace: Independent of the scientific critique above, this claim also fails our post-hoc test on structural grounds. The numerical values match observed magnetic pole field strengths published for decades (trivial-transcription test, Part 3.1b). No legitimate-novelty carve-out applies: the fitted coefficients are not obscure, and the dome provides no derivation that would constitute genuine prediction. Note that WIN-053 has no monitor.py domain at all — it appears on the predictions site as static prose, so the standard timestamping test does not apply here. See Part 3.1b.
patches.rationale: c4 curmudgeon H1 (major): boilerplate about "hardcoded expected value / live fetch call" is factually wrong for WIN-053 which has no monitor.py monitoring. Removes the factual error; preserves post-hoc critique on the correct grounds (trivial-transcription, not timestamping).
c4 curmudgeon H3 (minor): IGRF-13 main field is degree n=13 = 195 Gauss coefficients. 168 is the degree-12 count. Fixes numerical error without weakening argument.

patches.find: ~58,000–60,000 nT at the north magnetic pole and ~65,000–67,000 nT at the south magnetic pole
patches.replace: ~54,000–56,000 nT at the North Magnetic Pole and ~65,000–67,000 nT at the South Magnetic Pole
patches.rationale: c4 curmudgeon H5 (minor): North Magnetic Pole total field per WMM2025/IGRF-13 is ~54-56 kT, not 58-60 kT. South pole range ~65-67 kT is correct. Fixes numeric overstatement without affecting the core argument.

patches.find: The 1.574c figure was published by Tesla in 1905 (US Patent 787412) and restated in his Colorado Springs Notes (Nikola Tesla Museum, Beograd 1978); the value has been in the scientific record for over a century.
patches.replace: The underlying Tesla velocity measurement (~1.5c; round-trip 0.08484 s) was recorded in Tesla's 1899 Colorado Springs experiments and published posthumously in the Nikola Tesla Museum's Colorado Springs Notes (Beograd 1978). Tesla's contemporaneous US Patent 787412 (1905) describes the same wave-propagation method and depicts a spherical Earth in its drawings. The value has been in the scientific record for over a century.
patches.rationale: c4 curmudgeon H1 (major): dome attributes 1.574c to 1899 Colorado Springs Notes, not 1905 patent. Also, Tesla died 1943 — he cannot have "restated" anything in 1978 (posthumous publication). Fix corrects attribution without weakening post-hoc verdict.

patches.find: The dome catalog adopts this published number verbatim
patches.replace: The dome catalog adopts this published value and re-expresses it as 1.574c using the dome disc diameter
patches.rationale: c4 curmudgeon H2 (moderate): "verbatim" is imprecise — dome uses v·t=d with dome disc_diameter=40,030 km to produce 1.574c from Tesla's 471,240 km/s (=1.5719c). Removes " verbatim" which gives defender an opening.

patches.find: a century-old patent value does not qualify
patches.replace: a century-old published velocity does not qualify
patches.rationale: c4 curmudgeon H3 (moderate): "patent value" is wrong per H1 fix — the value originates in 1899 notes, not the patent. Change to "published velocity" is consistent and low-cost.

patches.find: the Fail-clause cannot be triggered without abandoning the Pass-clause's framing
patches.replace: triggering the Fail-clause would require abandoning the Pass-clause's framing of what counts as a signal
patches.rationale: c2 curmudgeon H6 (minor): EXP-191 conceded softening from "logically impossible" to "in tension with" — but body text still uses "cannot be triggered" which is the strong form. Replacing with "would require abandoning" is softer and consistent with the EXP-191 concession.

patches.replace: Genuinely prospective (dome registration 2026-03-12 via Git + OpenTimestamps; our tracker entry 2026-04-04; window closes 2026-08-12, verdict pending)
patches.rationale: c2 curmudgeon H8 (minor): "registered 2026-04-04" is our predictions.json tracker date. Per raw-text/03-predictions.txt dome OpenTimestamps-anchored on 2026-03-12. Fix distinguishes dome date from our tracker date, preventing a fair-minded reader from mistakenly concluding we chose the registration date.
